title: Best VPN Services
description: Daily-updated rankings of the best VPN services, scored on privacy, speed, server network, ease of use, and price.

competitors.name: Mullvad VPN
competitors.description: No-account anonymous VPN with flat-rate pricing and no usage logs

competitors.name: ProtonVPN
competitors.description: Swiss-based VPN with open-source apps and strong transparency record

competitors.name: IVPN
competitors.description: Privacy-first VPN with multi-hop routing and no tracking

competitors.name: NordVPN
competitors.description: 6,400+ servers in 111 countries with Meshnet and Threat Protection

competitors.name: ExpressVPN
competitors.description: Lightway protocol VPN with consistent speeds across 105 countries

competitors.name: Surfshark
competitors.description: Unlimited devices with CleanWeb ad blocking and Nexus network routing

competitors.name: Windscribe
competitors.description: Generous free tier with R.O.B.E.R.T. DNS-level blocker and 10 Gbit servers

competitors.name: AirVPN
competitors.description: Open-source, community-driven VPN with port forwarding and Eddie client

competitors.name: Private Internet Access
competitors.description: Court-proven no-logs policy with 35,000 servers and open-source apps

competitors.name: CyberGhost
competitors.description: User-friendly with 9,700+ servers and dedicated streaming/torrenting servers

history.commentary: The VPN market in 2026 increasingly splits between privacy-first services and mainstream speed-focused options. Mullvad retains #1 with its unique no-account system — users pay anonymously with cash or crypto and receive a random account number. This radical privacy approach, combined with its flat €5/month pricing (no discounts, no lock-ins), earns top marks. ProtonVPN moves to #2 after its 2025 security audit cleared all critical findings. IVPN holds #3 for power users who need multi-hop routing and minimum data footprint. NordVPN at #4 leads the mainstream segment with 6,400+ servers. Notably, ExpressVPN's ownership by Kape Technologies (also owns PIA and CyberGhost) continues to concern privacy advocates, capping its ranking despite excellent speeds.
highlights: Mullvad #1: no accounts, flat pricing, strongest privacy model
ProtonVPN rises to #2 after clean 2025 security audit
NordVPN #4 leads mainstream segment with 9,500+ servers
Kape Technologies owns ExpressVPN, PIA, and CyberGhost — conflict of interest noted
Windscribe free tier remains best value for light users
